vibe: Chill walking vibes for shopping around Daikanyama & Ebisu. City pop, lo-fi, Japanese indie, smooth grooves.

tracks.name: SPARKLE
tracks.artist: ビクター・ファンタスティック・オーケストラ
tracks.reason: Tatsuro Yamashita's city pop anthem — the sound of Japanese summer, sunshine, and driving with the windows down.

tracks.name: プライマル
tracks.reason: Tokyo Incidents (Sheena Ringo's band) at their most groovy. Art-rock meets jazz-funk — pure Shibuya energy.

tracks.name: Climax Night
tracks.reason: Yogee New Waves define the modern Japanese indie sound. Breezy, nostalgic, like a warm evening on the Meguro River.

tracks.name: Drop
tracks.reason: Cornelius is Shibuya-kei royalty. This track is a sonic collage — headphones recommended, every listen reveals something new.

tracks.name: Feather (feat. Cise Starr & Akin from CYNE)
tracks.artist: Nujabes, Cise Starr & Akin from CYNE
tracks.reason: Nujabes' masterpiece. The godfather of lo-fi hip-hop, gone too soon. This track is a Tokyo night walk in audio form.

tracks.name: Feather (feat. Cise Starr & Akin from CYNE)
tracks.artist: Nujabes, Cise Starr & Akin from CYNE
tracks.reason: Nujabes' masterpiece. The godfather of lo-fi hip-hop, gone too soon. This track is a Tokyo night walk in audio form.

tracks.name: STAY TUNE
tracks.reason: Suchmos brought Japanese city funk back to the mainstream. Smooth, confident, utterly cool.

tracks.name: NEW ERA
tracks.reason: Nulbarich is Tokyo's answer to Jamiroquai — acid jazz meets J-pop with effortless swagger.

tracks.name: Orphans
tracks.reason: cero make music that sounds like a Haruki Murakami novel feels. Complex, jazzy, dreamy Tokyo pop.

tracks.name: LOOP
tracks.reason: SIRUP is Japanese R&B at its silkiest. Bilingual, smooth, perfect walking-through-Daikanyama energy.

tracks.name: Kaiju no Hanauta
tracks.reason: Vaundy is Gen-Z Japan's biggest genre-hopper. This one's playful and catchy — J-pop with real musical depth.

tracks.name: It Runs Through Me (feat. De La Soul)
tracks.reason: Tom Misch + De La Soul is a match made in heaven. Jazzy guitar, golden-era hip-hop, pure sunshine.

tracks.name: Friday Morning
tracks.reason: Khruangbin at their most meditative. Thai funk meets Texas psychedelia — sounds like a slow morning in any beautiful city.

tracks.name: Maria También
tracks.reason: Khruangbin's Latin side — dreamy, hypnotic, and perfect for wandering narrow streets with no destination.

tracks.name: Evan Finds the Third Room
tracks.reason: Khruangbin's dub-funk deep cut. Mark Speer's guitar tone is liquid gold.

tracks.name: Some Feeling
tracks.reason: Mild Orange from New Zealand make dream-pop that feels like floating. Warm, hazy, gorgeous.

tracks.name: Tailwhip
tracks.reason: Men I Trust are Montreal dream-pop perfection. Emmanuella's voice over that bass line is instant calm.

tracks.name: Norton Commander (All We Need)
tracks.reason: More Men I Trust — this one's got a slightly darker groove. Still dreamy, but with more pulse.

tracks.name: Chamber Of Reflection
tracks.reason: Mac DeMarco's lo-fi masterpiece. Built on a Shigeo Sekito sample — the Japan connection comes full circle.

tracks.name: So Many Details
tracks.reason: Toro y Moi invented chillwave, basically. This track is a warm bath of synths and nostalgia.

tracks.name: Freelance
tracks.reason: Toro y Moi's funkier side. Disco-tinged, feel-good, strutting-down-the-street energy.

tracks.name: Almost Blue
tracks.reason: Chet Baker's voice is heartbreak distilled. A jazz standard that turns any coffee shop into a movie scene.

tracks.name: Porcelain
tracks.reason: Ichiko Aoba makes music that sounds like it's coming from another dimension. Japanese folk meets ambient — fragile and stunning.

tracks.name: WEATHER REPORT
tracks.reason: Fishmans are Japan's most beloved cult band. Dream-pop meets dub meets something entirely their own. Essential.

tracks.name: THANK YOU feat.NAVASHA DAYA (Sleep Walker replay Version) - Sleep Walker Replay Version
tracks.reason: Shuya Okino (Kyoto Jazz Massive) reimagined through Sleep Walker's broken-beat lens. Deep, soulful, and thoroughly Japanese club music.

tracks.name: Diving into Minds
tracks.reason: Soichi Terada's deep house is pure Tokyo underground. Warm, organic, endlessly groovy.

tracks.name: owari no kisetsu
tracks.reason: Rei Harakami was a Japanese electronic pioneer. Glitchy, beautiful, like music made of light and circuits. RIP.

tracks.name: ﾗﾋﾞﾘﾝｽ - Album Mix-Vocal:満島ひかり
tracks.reason: Mondo Grosso + Hikari Mitsushima = Japanese dance music perfection. The vocal is haunting, the beat is relentless.

tracks.name: Sit At The Piano
tracks.reason: Kan Sano sitting at the piano doing what he does best — making simple chords sound like a whole universe.

tracks.name: Detroit Part II
tracks.reason: Shigeto bridges Ann Arbor and Tokyo with this one. Broken beat meets jazz — his Japanese-American heritage in audio form.

tracks.name: GLOWED UP
tracks.reason: Kaytranada + Anderson .Paak is an unstoppable combo. Bouncy, funky, impossible to sit still.

tracks.name: Ylang Ylang
tracks.reason: FKJ live-loops his way through jazz, house, and soul. This extended jam is a journey — perfect for a long walk.

tracks.name: Stratus
tracks.reason: Uyama Hiroto (Nujabes collaborator) carrying the legacy forward. Jazz-hop that floats above the city.

tracks.name: Luv Letter
tracks.reason: DJ Okawari's piano-driven instrumental hip-hop. Gentle, melodic, like a letter you read on a bench in a quiet park.

tracks.name: Cosmic Perspective
tracks.reason: Mndsgn makes beats that sound like they're melting. LA beat scene meets cosmic jazz — Stones Throw excellence.

tracks.name: Presence I
tracks.reason: STUTS, Takako Matsu, and KID FRESINO on one track. This is modern Tokyo distilled into 4 minutes.
